name: Crescent Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering Certificates of Deposit, auto loans, and basic banking services with online account management and a mobile app.
description_long:
Crescent Bank is a traditional FDIC-insured banking institution that serves consumers seeking deposit products and auto financing solutions. The bank is a Member FDIC, meaning all eligible deposits are protected up to $250,000 per depositor per ownership type, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. government. The institution operates physical branch locations and provides digital banking channels including a mobile app and online portal.

The bank's primary offerings include Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with competitive rates—currently advertised at 3.65% APY on 12-month terms—positioning them as higher-yield alternatives to standard savings and money market accounts. Crescent also originates and services auto loans, allowing borrowers to access loan information, set up automatic payments, and manage payment reminders through their online platform. The bank provides standard online and mobile banking capabilities, though specific checking and savings account details are not prominently featured on their website. The institution also operates an auto dealer program, suggesting lending relationships with dealership partners.

Crescent Bank distinguishes itself through its focus on CD products with rates competitive enough to highlight on their homepage, and a unified auto finance division (Crescent Auto Finance) designed to streamline customer support. The bank emphasizes FDIC insurance education and maintains physical branch locations, suggesting a hybrid banking model combining traditional and digital services. Their mobile app availability indicates investment in customer digital accessibility.

As a regional bank with limited online presence regarding checking/savings products, Crescent appears positioned for consumers prioritizing FDIC safety and CD yields over extensive product variety. The reliance on auto loan servicing as a prominent business line suggests this may be a significant revenue driver. Consumers should note that detailed account terms, fee structures, and full product information require direct contact or deeper website navigation beyond publicly featured content. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with multiple term options and stated 3.65% APY on 12-month products
FDIC insurance up to $250,000 per depositor per ownership type on covered accounts
Auto loan origination and underwriting through dealership partnerships
Online auto loan account access and information portal
Automatic payment setup and management for auto loans
Payment reminder services for auto loan borrowers
Mobile banking app for account access and transactions
Physical branch locations for in-person banking services
Online payment processing for auto loans
Crescent Auto Finance branded auto lending division
Checking and Savings accounts (implied through FDIC coverage description, details limited)
pros: FDIC insured member bank with deposits protected up to $250,000 per ownership category
Competitive CD rates advertised at 3.65% APY on 12-month terms at time of website review
Mobile app available for account management and banking convenience
Online auto loan management with automatic payment setup and payment reminders
Physical branch locations available for in-person banking services
Educational resources on FDIC insurance coverage and protection limits
Auto financing program integrated with auto dealer partnerships for streamlined lending
cons: Limited information on website regarding checking and savings account products, rates, and features
No published fee schedule or detailed account terms visible on primary website pages
Auto loan program undergoing rebranding transition (Crescent Auto Finance as of 9/15/2025) with potential service disruption concerns
Sparse details on customer service availability, response times, or support channels beyond branch locations
No comparative advantage evident versus larger national banks on deposit product offerings
best_for: CD investors seeking FDIC-insured savings with competitive yields and flexible term options
Auto loan customers in regions served by Crescent Bank branches preferring local banking relationships
Borrowers purchasing vehicles through dealership partners offering Crescent financing
Consumers prioritizing FDIC protection and traditional bank safety over fintech innovation
diagnosis: Crescent Bank is best suited for CD investors prioritizing FDIC safety and competitive yields, and auto borrowers in their service regions seeking local lending relationships. Primary caveat: the bank's website provides minimal detail on checking/savings products and complete account terms, requiring direct contact for comprehensive product comparison and transparent pricing information.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
